title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 42 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Spain's official start to the new year, celebrated nationwide with fireworks, family gatherings, and the tradition of eating twelve grapes at midnight.

festivals.name: Epiphany / Día de Reyes
festivals.description: Spain's most important gift-giving holiday honoring the Three Kings, featuring grand parades and the traditional Roscón de Reyes cake.

festivals.name: Seville Carnival
festivals.description: One of Spain's most vibrant carnivals with elaborate costumes, satirical groups (comparsas), and days of dancing and celebration throughout the city.

festivals.name: Tenerife Carnival
festivals.description: The second-largest carnival in the world with colorful parades, costumes, music, and street performances rivaling Rio de Janeiro.

festivals.name: Carnival of Cádiz
festivals.description: A historic carnival known for its witty satirical groups (comparsas) and chirigotas that poke fun at current events and politics.

festivals.name: Las Fallas
festivals.description: A spectacular festival featuring enormous satirical papier-mâché sculptures (fallas) that are set on fire in the climactic Nit del Foc, with parades and fireworks throughout.

festivals.name: Good Friday / Viernes Santo
festivals.description: A national public holiday marked by solemn religious processions, especially dramatic in Andalusian cities like Seville with centuries-old traditions.

festivals.name: Easter Monday
festivals.description: A national public holiday celebrated throughout Spain with family gatherings, church services, and traditional Easter sweets.

festivals.name: San Jorge Festival
festivals.description: Catalonia's celebration of Sant Jordi with a tradition of exchanging roses and books, filling Barcelona's streets with color and literary spirit.

festivals.name: Labor Day
festivals.description: Spain's national public holiday honoring workers with parades, demonstrations, and celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: Festival of San Isidro
festivals.description: Madrid's patron saint festival featuring bullfights, concerts, theater, and street parties throughout the capital.

festivals.name: Romería de El Rocío
festivals.description: A massive pilgrimage of nearly a million people traveling on foot, horse, and cart to the Doñana wetlands to honor the Virgin of El Rocío.

festivals.name: Sónar Barcelona
festivals.description: One of Europe's premier electronic music and multimedia festivals attracting top DJs and artists from around the world.

festivals.name: Festa de Sant Joan
festivals.description: Catalonia's midsummer celebration with bonfires, fireworks, and the traditional Coca de Recapte pastry enjoyed throughout the region.

festivals.name: Bonfires of San Juan
festivals.description: A Valencian tradition featuring bonfires on beaches, parades, and street parties celebrating midsummer with fireworks and revelry.

festivals.name: Assumption of Mary
festivals.description: A major national public holiday marking the Assumption, celebrated with religious services and summer holidays throughout Spain.

festivals.name: Running of the Bulls (San Fermín)
festivals.description: Pamplona's world-famous festival featuring the iconic encierro where bulls chase participants through streets, plus bullfights, concerts, and parades.

festivals.name: Vitoria Festival
festivals.description: Vitoria's week-long celebration featuring concerts, theater, street performances, and Basque cultural displays with the famous white and red costume tradition.

festivals.name: Moros y Cristianos
festivals.description: A colorful festival reenacting the historical battles between Moors and Christians with elaborate parades, costumes, and fireworks.

festivals.name: Bilbao BBK Live
festivals.description: A major international music festival featuring rock, indie, electronic, and hip-hop artists across multiple stages in the Basque countryside.

festivals.name: Benicàssim Festival
festivals.description: A famous Mediterranean music festival attracting international indie and electronic artists with a beach setting.

festivals.name: National Day / Día Nacional de España
festivals.description: Spain's national holiday celebrating the founding of the Spanish nation with military parades, flag ceremonies, and official celebrations throughout the country.

festivals.name: La Tomatina
festivals.description: A world-renowned festival where participants throw overripe tomatoes at each other in a massive food fight that turns streets red.

festivals.name: Aste Nagusia (Big Week)
festivals.description: Bilbao's grand week-long celebration featuring concerts, traditional Basque sports (txuleta), gastronomic events, and fireworks.

festivals.name: Veranos de la Villa
festivals.description: Madrid's summer arts festival offering free and paid theater, music, dance, and cinema events throughout the city.

festivals.name: Diada de Catalunya
festivals.description: Catalonia's national holiday commemorating the 1714 siege with political demonstrations, parades, and displays of regional pride.

festivals.name: Oyster Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Galician seafood culture featuring oyster tastings, traditional music, and culinary demonstrations.

festivals.name: Halloween/Samhain Celebrations
festivals.description: Increasingly celebrated in Spain with themed parties, costume events, and street celebrations, particularly in major cities.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: A national public holiday honoring the dead with visits to cemeteries, traditional all-saints bread, and family gatherings.

festivals.name: Castanyada
festivals.description: A Catalan tradition on All Saints' Day involving roasted chestnuts and sweet potato (boniato) shared with family and friends.

festivals.name: Constitution Day
festivals.description: Spain's national holiday celebrating the 1978 Constitution with official ceremonies, parades, and flag displays throughout the country.

festivals.name: Christmas Markets and Fairs
festivals.description: Holiday markets selling traditional crafts, toys, and decorations throughout Spain's cities and towns during the festive season.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: Spain's national public holiday celebrated with family gatherings, church services, and traditional festive meals.

festivals.name: Lucía Festival
festivals.description: A pre-Christmas celebration in Asturias honoring Saint Lucy with traditional music, dancing, and local festivities.

festivals.name: Fire Festival (Iruak)
festivals.description: A winter solstice celebration in the Basque Country with bonfires, traditional rituals, and community gatherings.

festivals.name: Nochevieja (New Year's Eve)
festivals.description: Spain's celebrated New Year's Eve with the famous grape-eating tradition at Puerta del Sol in Madrid and celebrations throughout the country.

festivals.name: Carnival of Orihuela
festivals.description: A Valencian carnival featuring satirical parades, colorful costumes, and the burning of effigies (judas) in traditional style.

festivals.name: Andalucía Day
festivals.description: Andalusia's regional holiday celebrating the region's autonomy with official ceremonies, concerts, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Melilla Autonomous City Day
festivals.description: Melilla's celebration of its autonomous status with parades, cultural events, and regional festivities.

festivals.name: Feria de Abril
festivals.description: Seville's spectacular spring fair featuring traditional Andalusian dress, flamenco dancing, horse parades, and aristocratic family pavilions.

festivals.name: Mercat Medieval de Montblanc
festivals.description: A medieval market festival recreating life in the Middle Ages with craftspeople, entertainers, and authentic period activities.

festivals.name: Festa Major de Gràcia
festivals.description: Barcelona's neighborhood festival celebrating Gràcia with decorated streets, outdoor concerts, performances, and parades.
